name: M'dirr
bio:
Physical Appearance: Khajiit male. Feline features characteristic of his race. Wears typical middle-class attire consisting of light brown linens, pigskin shoes, and a brown shirt - respectable clothing for a village resident. Carries pieces of light armor, which he equips during times of crisis and continues wearing afterward.
Race: Khajiit
Personality Keywords: superstitious, fearful, simple
Role: M'dirr is a common villager living in Border Watch, a small Khajiit settlement on Cyrodiil's border. He shares his house with Tsramla. Like all his fellow townsfolk, he firmly believes in the K'sharra prophecy, which states that three specific signs will serve as warning before the world ends.
Home Location Information: Border Watch village, where M'dirr shares a house with Tsramla.
Daily Routine - Normal Times: M'dirr sleeps from 10pm until 4am, then wanders around town throughout the day. At 11am, he takes a two-hour lunch break at the Border Watch Inn in the southwestern part of the village before returning to his wandering.
Daily Routine - During Crisis: When the three signs of K'sharra are manifested, M'dirr runs around town in fear and panic. During this time, he equips his weapon and any armor he possesses. He continues this panicked behavior until the crisis is resolved.
Equipment: M'dirr is armed with a blunt weapon of the best possible quality, which may be enchanted. He carries up to five pieces of light armor (excluding cuirass), each of which may also be enchanted. He also carries a small amount of gold. After experiencing the fulfillment of the K'sharra prophecy, he wears his armor constantly, including while sleeping.
The K'sharra Prophecy: M'dirr believes in the K'sharra prophecy - that three signs will herald the end of the world. When asked about the prophecy, M'dirr will direct people to speak with the town shaman, Ri'Bassa.
Personality - The Frightened Villager: M'dirr is a Khajiit of very few words who follows a regular routine of wandering the village and taking meals at the local inn. When the K'sharra prophecy appears to be fulfilled, he runs around town in fear and panic, abandoning his normal activities. After the crisis passes, he returns to his usual routine but continues wearing his armor at all times, even while sleeping.
author: Bethesda
